rulings.Case Title: LUCAS SCHENCK VS. ST. PAUL SURPLUS LINES INSURANCE COMPANY, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 30, 2010, line 11. DEFENDANT ACCEPTANCE INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. The insurance contract includes a standard "use" clause and the court is presented with a pure question of law. The predominating cause/substantial factor test is favored. (See State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Co. v. Grisham (2004) 122 Cal. App. 4th 563). The use of vehicle did not contribute to the injury. No reasonable probability that the defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)
